key_insight: A fully variational CutFEM for finite-strain elasticity can be built from one augmented energy, with Nitsche boundary terms and ghost-penalty stabilization, while automatic differentiation supplies stresses and tangents for any hyperelastic model.
engineering_relevance: This gives a robust unfitted FEM workflow for complex geometries and moving boundaries, with cut-independent stability and reliable Newton solves in solid mechanics.
method: Augment the bulk hyperelastic energy with weak Dirichlet/Neumann enforcement via Nitsche terms.
Add ghost-penalty stabilization to maintain coercivity and good conditioning on cut cells.
Use automatic differentiation to generate the first Piola-Kirchhoff stress and elasticity tensor from the scalar energy density.
evidence_signal: The abstract reports optimal h-convergence for linear, quadratic, and cubic elements on a smooth test case, plus cut-independent coercivity, continuity, and an O(h^-2) condition number bound.
watch_item: Accuracy is still limited by corner singularities at mixed Dirichlet–Neumann junctions unless local mesh refinement is used.
paper_title: A Unified CutFEM Formulation for Finite-Strain Elasticity: Energy Minimisation and Corner Singularities
